result.summary: custhelp.com exposes Oracle B2C Service REST, SOAP, and Element Manager public REST APIs; all require authentication via Oracle account/session auth or SSO JWT, with no separate GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surface found.

oracle_account_password.label: Oracle B2C Service account credentials
oracle_account_password.setup: Use the Oracle account username and password for the site. Create or manage the account through the site’s Oracle identity/account flows linked from the portal, such as [Create an Account](https://profile.oracle.com/myprofile/account/create-account.jspx) or the sign-in flow from the support portal. These credentials are used directly for some Connect SOAP authentication flows and, via a helper script, can be exchanged for a session token for REST.

oracle_sso_jwt.label: Oracle B2C Service SSO JWT
oracle_sso_jwt.setup: Configure an external IdP for the site, then mint an RS256 JWT with the configured identity provider values. For Element Manager public APIs, Oracle’s article says to enable an external IdP in the site, add an OAuth/SSO configuration, and generate a JWT with claims like `sub` (an SSO-enabled agent account), `iss` (the provider entity ID), `aud` (the configured audience such as `/authToken`), `jti`, and `exp`, signed with the private key matching the uploaded certificate.

surfaces.name: Oracle B2C Service Connect REST API
Oracle B2C Service Connect Web Services for SOAP API
Oracle B2C Service Element Manager Public REST API
